understanding.goal_understanding: Guide the controllable piece(s) on a 3-pixels-per-cell grid toward level objectives (typically reaching goal tiles/areas) within a limited step budget, potentially using multiple selectable pieces to progress.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: MOVE actions shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked; blocking can occur from adjacent walls/boundaries or other objects, and the 'blocked' hint is only a heuristic. Some adjacent objects are pushable, so moving into them can push them along if space/constraints allow. Levels may contain multiple selectable pieces, and CLICK on a selectable’s exact pixel/grid position switches control to that piece (as seen when clicking [33, 21] changed the selected piece from the one at [15, 21] to the one at [33, 21]).

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All adjacent directions are flagged as walls; try moving right to test whether the wall is actually impassable or has special behavior.
history.reasoning: All adjacent directions are flagged as walls; try moving right to test whether the wall is actually impassable or has special behavior.

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls; test whether the right wall is passable or allows sliding/overlap by attempting to move right.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls; test whether the right wall is passable or allows sliding/overlap by attempting to move right.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in by walls on three sides; try moving into the adjacent selectable on the right to test if it can be pushed or if a swap occurs.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on three sides; try moving into the adjacent selectable on the right to test if it can be pushed or if a swap occurs.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | We’re enclosed by walls on all sides, so try switching control to the other selectable to see if it has a path to the goal.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls on all sides, so try switching control to the other selectable to see if it has a path to the goal.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Right is adjacent to the goal area; attempt to move into it to test whether it accepts the selectable and completes the level.
history.reasoning: Right is adjacent to the goal area; attempt to move into it to test whether it accepts the selectable and completes the level.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_UP | We appear enclosed by walls on all sides; test whether moving up allows overlap into the non-collidable goal area or reveals a special boundary interaction.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls on all sides; test whether moving up allows overlap into the non-collidable goal area or reveals a special boundary interaction.
